name: Cam Repeater
title: Digital Periscopes
desc:
A webapp that simulates a "periscope" by placing a series of tablets in a row; or dynamic feedback loops when placed in a ring formation. Every tablet displays small AR marker in each corner of the screen. Once the next tablet "sees" the markers, it stretches and distorts the image to fill its own screen. Even though the image degrades over distance, each tablet repaints the markers so that the next screen in the sequnce can detect them easily.

I experimented with a few different types of tablets but found that for covering large distances it was important to have access to the camera's PTZ (Pan/Tilt/Zoom) controls. This kept the image quality higher and meant I could extend the array of tablets further.

Another interesting side effect is the slight delay as the image from the camera is rendered to the screen. In the second half of the video you'll see that by setting up a ring of screens we were able to "trap" light inside an infinite loop. Unfortunately the camera firmware would automatically adjust the image for better contrast so all the pixels in the loop quickly make their way to either 100% white or 100% black. We were still able to create some interesting effects by turning out all the lights and flashing bright lights into the cameras. That image was mostly black and white already so it was possible to observe blobs of light loop through the ring a few times before fading out.

name: Amex DLS
title: Bootstrap for Amex Digital Properties
desc:
American Express has a great deal of websites, microsites, apps and other digital media and wanted to harmonize the look and feel for all of them. Not an easy task when they had over 1,000 developers across the world constantly building and updating all of them using their own individual JavaScript and Stylesheets. They approached B-Reel to help find a solution and that was the genesis of their "Design Language System" which we built and they continue to evolve.

It consists of American Express wanted a "Design Language System" for all of its digital properties including websites, apps and . They asked B-Reel's development team to create the library to be used by thousands of their front-end developers and UI designers. It included the usual fonts, styles, and colors, but also extended into widgets, animations, and any other digital aspects of their brand.

A gigantic repository created for American Express consisting of...
